description_long: This object consists of six pole-mounted traffic lights arranged in two rows, each with a sturdy metallic pole and a rectangular base for stability. The signal heads are encased in black housings and are mounted on the poles at varying heights, with each signal featuring three circular lenses stacked vertically. Behind each signal head is a large, rectangular, orange backplate with the word 'LEFT' printed in black, enhancing visibility and indicating a dedicated left-turn signal. Some poles are equipped with visible control boxes attached beneath the signal heads, likely housing the electronics for signal operation. The overall construction appears robust, designed to withstand outdoor urban environments and provide clear directional guidance to drivers.
description: A group of six pole-mounted traffic lights with orange backplates labeled 'LEFT', each featuring three vertically stacked signal lenses and some with attached control boxes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the orange 'LEFT' backplates and the arrangement of the six traffic lights in two rows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the signal heads, the vertical stacking of the lights, and the attached control boxes on some poles.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the orange backplates and the supporting poles, with less detail of the signal faces.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the profile of the traffic lights, the mounting hardware, and the control boxes.
